title: Advisory Wait State
description: Read-only advisory-wait evidence snapshot for a PR head.

outcome.description: Shared routing outcome consumed by E14, F2, and the shell-fallback path (AW1-AW5). On REQUEST_NEEDED, F2 returns to E14 while E14 itself requests Copilot and polls; F3 reads f3Outcome instead.

f3Outcome.description: F3-authoritative outcome; treats copilotPending=false as SATISFIED so F3 may merge a settled-but-not-re-reviewed advisory state. F3 uses this field exclusively when helper output is valid, which is why it can diverge from outcome.

secondaryBotLogin.description: Resolved OPTIONAL secondary advisory bot login (lower-cased), or empty when no secondary is configured. Non-gating supplement; never satisfies the primary advisory-wait gate.

secondaryRequestNeeded.description: True when the configured secondary advisory bot should be requested once for the current HEAD because the primary is cap-exhausted or stalled/rate-limited. Non-gating: never alters outcome/f3Outcome and posts no advisory-wait marker.

copilotRecovery.description: #1572 terminal Copilot stall-recovery state. Deliberately independent of outcome/f3Outcome above: no field here is derived from them, and no field above is derived from this object. `state: "COPILOT_UNAVAILABLE"` is waiver *eligibility* only (see idd-advisory-wait.instructions.md) -- it must never be read as advisory satisfaction or readiness by any consumer.

completedCycleCount.description: Count of trusted, active-claim-bound, current-HEAD-bound advisory-recovery markers. A completed cycle is counted by marker presence, never by the largest embedded attempt number (diagnostic only).

terminalWindowMinutes.description: Configured terminal-unavailability window in minutes (advisoryWait.terminalWindow, default 720 = 12h).

clockAnchor.description: GitHub created_at of the earliest trusted, bound, current-HEAD advisory-recovery marker; empty when none exists. Embedded marker timestamps never move this anchor -- only the GitHub-assigned comment created_at counts.

elapsedMinutes.description: Minutes between clockAnchor and now; 0 when there is no anchor.

windowElapsed.description: clockAnchor is non-empty AND elapsedMinutes >= terminalWindowMinutes.

activeClaimProvided.description: True only when both --claim-id and --agent-id were supplied to bind the active claim. False fails the whole object closed to NOT_TERMINAL, reason: active-claim-not-provided.

state.description: COPILOT_UNAVAILABLE only when the recovery-cycle cap is exhausted, the terminal window has elapsed since clockAnchor, and no current-HEAD Copilot review exists (lastCopilotCommit !== prHeadSha) -- all proven from trusted evidence. Any missing or ambiguous evidence fails closed to NOT_TERMINAL.

reason.description: Machine-readable reason for the current state: active-claim-not-provided, no-trusted-recovery-markers, recovery-cap-not-exhausted, terminal-window-not-elapsed, current-head-review-exists, or the COPILOT_UNAVAILABLE satisfied-all-conditions reason.

staleRequestRecovery.description: #1571 bounded stale-request recovery eligibility. Independent of outcome/f3Outcome and of copilotRecovery above: distinguishes the unproven-coverage (copilotPendingCoversHead=false) case PR #1562 identified from the pre-existing RECOVERY_NEEDED/AW3-R marker-anchor-only path (copilotPendingCoversHead=true). Consumed only by E14's REQUEST_NEEDED-with-pending sub-path (AW3-S) to decide whether the bounded remove/re-request/verify/mark cycle may run.

action.description: 'attempt': unproven pending request, no same-head marker, recovery-cycle budget remains -- run AW3-S. 'cap-exhausted': same unproven condition, but the independent #1572 recovery-cycle cap is exhausted for this HEAD -- do not mutate; fall back to CAP_EXHAUSTED_ROUTE. 'not-applicable': no stale-request recovery decision applies.

reason.description: Machine-readable reason: not-pending, same-head-marker-present, proven-covers-head, recovery-cap-exhausted, or recovery-attempt-eligible.
